questions.question: Given g(x) = x² + 1, find g(−3).
questions.explanation: Substitute x = −3 into g(x) = x² + 1. g(−3) = (−3)² + 1 = 9 + 1 = 10.

questions.question: If f(x) = 2x + 5 and g(x) = x − 3, what is f(g(2))?
The function h(x) = 5 − x is reflected across the x-axis. Write the equation of the reflected function.
questions.explanation: Reflecting across the x-axis means multiply the output by −1. The reflected function is −h(x) = −(5 − x) = x − 5.

questions.question: If f(x) = 4x, what is the output when the input is 1.5?
Given f(x) = x/2 + 3, solve f(x) = 8.
questions.explanation: Set x/2 + 3 = 8. Then x/2 = 5, so x = 10.
